query: During active times, how energetic is the ring current?

row.paper: Zhao, J.T., et al., 2022. Observational evidence of ring current in the magnetosphere of Mercury. Nature Communications, 13(1), p.924. DOI:10.1038/s41467-022-28521-3
row.query: During active times, how energetic is the ring current?

tool_input.summary: PPI does not host modern dedicated ring-current missions (e.g., THEMIS/RBSP), but it does include Earth flyby intervals from Galileo with energetic particle (EPD), plasma (PLS), and magnetic field (MAG) reduced products that can be used to quantify storm-time/ring-current particle energies during the 1990 and 1992 Earth encounters.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-EPD-4-SUMM-EARTH1-15MIN-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Energetic Particle Detector (EPD) 15‑minute summaries during the 1990 Earth flyby; suitable for estimating storm-time particle energy spectra/fluxes relevant to ring-current energetics.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-EPD-4-SUMM-EARTH2-15MIN-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Same as above for the 1992 Earth flyby; provides a second active-time interval for energetic particle/ring-current analysis.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-PLS-4-SUMM-EARTH1-ET-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Plasma instrument (PLS) summary products for the 1990 Earth flyby; useful for lower-energy (eV–keV) populations that connect to ring-current source/injection conditions.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-PLS-4-SUMM-EARTH2-ET-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PLS summary products for the 1992 Earth flyby; complements EPD and MAG for storm-time characterization.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-MAG-3-RDR-EARTH1-HIGHRES-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: High-resolution magnetic field (MAG) reduced data during the 1990 Earth flyby; provides geomagnetic context needed to relate particle enhancements to storm-time ring-current signatures.

candidates.title: Galileo at Earth: GO-E-MAG-3-RDR-EARTH2-HIGHRES-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: High-resolution magnetic field (MAG) reduced data during the 1992 Earth flyby; supports ring-current/geomagnetic-activity linkage for that interval.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
This node has NO mission layer. ~767 datasets sit directly under data/.
Do NOT call pds_list_missions — it will return an empty list.
Instead, go directly to pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') using mission or instrument keywords to narrow the listing.
Then probe the most relevant directories with pds_probe_datasets.


Common mission/instrument keywords:
  Cassini → cassini (instruments: CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, UVIS, INMS, CDA, RADAR)
  Galileo → galileo (instruments: EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR)
  Juno → juno (instruments: Waves, JADE, JEDI, MAG, MWR, UVS)
  Voyager 1/2 → voyager1, voyager2 (instruments: CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS)
  New Horizons → new-horizons (instruments: PEPSSI, SWAP, SDC)
  Pioneer 10/11 → pioneer10, pioneer11 (instruments: CPI, GTT, MAG, PA)
  MESSENGER → messenger (instruments: EPPS, MAG, NS)
  Ulysses → ulysses (instruments: SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, SWICS, COSPIN)
  MAVEN → maven (instruments: MAG, NGIMS, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, LPW)
  THEMIS → themis (instruments: ESA, SST, FGM, SCM, EFI)

Dataset naming: directories often follow the pattern '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<description>' (e.g. 'cassini-caps-calibrated').
Use the filter parameter on list_dataset_dirs to search by mission or instrument name.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') to find datasets.
  Step 2: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths.
  Step 3: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections.
  Step 4: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
During active times, how energetic is the ring current?

VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-EPD-4-SUMM-EARTH1-15MIN-V1.0 data set from 1990-312T17:21:55 to 1990-351T23:30:42.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-EPD-4-SUMM-EARTH2-15MIN-V1.0 data set from 1992-11-06T17:01:10.000 to 1992-12-18T15:08:38.000.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-MAG-3-RDR-EARTH1-HIGHRES-V1.0 data set from 1990-11-05T16:31:18.233 to 1990-12-31T18:19:53.196.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-MAG-3-RDR-EARTH2-HIGHRES-V1.0 data set from 1992-11-03T00:48:11.315 to 1992-12-19T02:39:51.910.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-PLS-4-SUMM-EARTH1-ET-V1.0 data set from 1990-312T23:22:19.807 to 1990-342T20:10:55.398.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-PLS-4-SUMM-EARTH2-ET-V1.0 data set from 1992-312T00:01:52.430 to 1992-351T23:58:46.225.

CERTIFIED This volume contains calibrated data from the NEAR Magnetometer for the year and daynumber range 1998-023 through 1998-026 (calendar dates 1998-01-23 through 1998-01-26). Cadence depends on telemetry rate - it varies from seconds to tens of seconds.
CERTIFIED This volume contains Galileo Orbiter at Earth from the GO-E-EPD-4-SUMM-EARTH1-15MIN-V1.0 data set from 1990-312T17:21:55 to 1990-351T23:30:42.
